XOnlineStatWrite

Sets attributes and ratings for one or more specified players.

Note    It is recommended that titles use XOnlineStatWriteEx instead.

HRESULT XOnlineStatWrite(
    DWORD dwNumStatSpecs,
    const XONLINE_STAT_SPEC *pStatSpecs,
    HANDLE hWorkEvent,
    PXONLINETASK_HANDLE phTask
);

Parameters

dwNumStatSpecs
[in] Number of XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structures in the array pointed to by the pStatSpecs parameter.
pStatSpecs
[in] Pointer to an array of XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structures. Each of these structures contains statistics for a particular player.
hWorkEvent
[in, optional] Handle to a caller-created event object. This object will be signaled when there is work to do by calling XOnlineTaskContinue. The caller may pass in NULL instead, indicating that the caller will poll XOnlineTaskContinue itself.
phTask

[out] Address of an XONLINETASK_HANDLE that receives the task that stores the statistics. This task sends the storage request to the statistics service and receives the response. This handle must be pumped to completion by XOnlineTaskContinue. Call XOnlineTaskClose to close the task.

Note    To throttle requests sent to the statistics service, XOnlineTaskContinue will return XONLINE_E_TASK_ABORTED_BY_DUPLICATE if the task is still being pumped when a second operation is started.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful.

If the function fails, it returns one of the following error codes.

Value Description
E_OUTOFMEMORY Insufficient memory. Free some memory.
Other See Online Errors for information about other possible errors.

The following table lists the possible values that XOnlineTaskContinue may return when called with the handle for the task created by XOnlineStatWrite.

Value Description
XONLINE_E_STAT_TOO_MANY_SPECS You are trying to set too many specs in this call. Send fewer specs per call.
XONLINE_E_STAT_TOO_MANY_STATS You are trying to write too many stats for a given player. Send fewer stats per player.
XONLINE_E_STAT_BAD_REQUEST The message could be empty, the attribute count could be zero, and so on. Check the values you are passing in. If they are all correct, this could indicate a problem on the server.
XONLINE_S_STAT_CAN_UPLOAD_ATTACHMENT The title may upload a file attachment for these statistics. The title should call XOnlineStatWriteGetResult to obtain a handle to a remote file reference to use when calling XOnlineStorageUpload.

Remarks

The XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structure allows you to specify multiple statistics for a specified user. A statistic may be the player's rating or the value of a title-defined attribute.

This function can be called only with direct user input related to statistics.

If a title supports stats with attachments, only a single player and leaderboard may be specified in a call to XOnlineStatWrite.

For maximum performance, the title should set all the results from a game session using a single call to the XOnlineStatWrite function. To prevent the statistics service from overloading, stat writes are automatically throttled by the XOnline library to a maximum of one request every 300 milliseconds, no matter how many times or how often XOnlineStatWrite is called. This function must not be called more often than once every five minutes between game sessions. During a game session, this function can be called only once at the beginning, once at the end, and every five minutes if the game session lasts longer than five minutes. This function should not be used to write to more than five leaderboards per player. Contact your technical game manager (TGM) or consult the Technical Certification Requirements for details.

The XOnlineStatWrite function can set statistics only for players that are logged on from the local Xbox console. Therefore, the function can set statistics for a maximum of four players.

The rank statistic is computed by the statistics service. Although the rank statistic can be retrieved by the XOnlineStatRead function, it cannot be set by the XOnlineStatWrite function.

Titles are limited to a maximum of 64 (XONLINE_STAT_MAX_STATS_IN_SPEC) unique statistics per player per leaderboard. This limit is for title-defined statistics only; rating and rank are not included in this limit. Titles are limited to a maximum of five per player (XONLINE_STAT_MAX_SPECS_IN_WRITE_REQUEST) XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structures per call to XOnlineStatWrite. The XOnlineStatWrite functions fails if the title attempts to write more than either limit.

Note    Leaderboards are also referred to as scoreboards.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

Xbox Live Functions | XOnlineStatRead, XOnlineStatReset, XONLINE_STAT
